articles.title: Luka Doncic's MVP Hopes Shift After 65‑Game Rule Relief

articles.summary: The NBA granted a 65‑game eligibility relief rule, which altered the landscape for MVP considerations. Luka Doncic, a leading candidate, now faces a changed timeline for his campaign. The adjustment could impact how voters evaluate his season performance.
key_points: NBA introduced a 65‑game eligibility relief rule.
The rule change affects Luka Doncic's MVP candidacy timeline.

key_points: NBA MVP finalists: Shai Gilgeous‑Alexander, Victor Wembanyma, Nikola Jokic.
Luka Doncic did not make the final three.

articles.title: Thunder dominate Suns 119-84 in Game 1

key_points: Thunder win Game 1 by 35 points, 119‑84.
Defending champions and top seed showcase offensive firepower.
Suns struggle to find rhythm, prompting calls for adjustments.

articles.title: LeBron’s all‑around effort fuels Lakers’ Game 1 upset of Rockets
articles.original_title: LeBron plays 'triple threat' to spring Game 1 upset
articles.summary: With Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves out, LeBron James delivered a versatile performance—19 points, 13 assists, 8 rebounds and 2 steals—to spark the Los Angeles Lakers’ upset win over the Houston Rockets in Game 1. James’ “triple‑threat” play kept the Lakers competitive despite missing key teammates.
key_points: LeBron records 19‑13‑8 line, leading Lakers to upset victory.
Absence of Doncic and Reaves forces James into a multi‑faceted role.
Lakers overcome early deficit to secure Game 1 win.
